Package coxprogramming.processing.sprites

Class Event

Object
Event
public class Event extends Object
A flexible event system that lets different parts of your game communicate with each other without needing to be directly connected.

The idea is simple: one object triggers an event (something happened), and any number of other objects that have been bound to that event will have one of their methods called automatically.

This pattern is sometimes called the Observer pattern â€” the event is the thing being watched, and the bound functions are the observers.

Example â€” a "coin collected" event that takes no arguments: 

   Event onCoinCollected = new Event();

   // In setup():
   onCoinCollected.bind(scoreBoard, "incrementScore");

   // When a coin is collected:
   onCoinCollected.trigger();

Example â€” an event that passes the number of points earned: 

   Event onScore = new Event(Integer.class);
   onScore.bind(hud, "showPoints");   // hud must have: void showPoints(int n)
   onScore.trigger(100);

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Event(Class<?>... args)
    Creates a new Event and declares what types of values it will pass to its listeners when triggered.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    bind(Object listener, String name)
    Attaches a listener function to this event so it will be called whenever trigger(Object...) is invoked.
    boolean
    bound(Object listener, String name)
    Returns true if the given function is currently bound to this event, or false if it is not.
    void
    trigger(Object... arguments)
    Fires the event, calling every bound listener function and passing arguments to each one.
    void
    unbind(Object listener, String name)
    Detaches a previously bound listener function from this event so it will no longer be called when the event fires.

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Event

      public Event(Class<?>... args)
      Creates a new Event and declares what types of values it will pass to its listeners when triggered.

      Use Java wrapper types to describe the parameter types (e.g. Integer.class for an int, Float.class for a float). If the event passes no values, call with no arguments: new Event().

      Parameters:
      args - the parameter types that will be passed when this event fires

  • Method Details

    • trigger

      public void trigger(Object... arguments)
      Fires the event, calling every bound listener function and passing arguments to each one. The types and order of arguments must match what was declared in the constructor.
      Parameters:
      arguments - the values to pass to each listener

    • bind

      public void bind(Object listener, String name)
      Attaches a listener function to this event so it will be called whenever trigger(Object...) is invoked.

      listener is the object that owns the function, and name is the exact name of the method as a String. The method's parameter types must match the types declared in the Event's constructor.

      The same function cannot be bound more than once â€” a warning is printed to the console if you try.

      Parameters:
      listener - the object that contains the method to call
      name - the name of the method to call

    • unbind

      public void unbind(Object listener, String name)
      Detaches a previously bound listener function from this event so it will no longer be called when the event fires. A warning is printed if the function was not already bound.
      Parameters:
      listener - the object that owns the method
      name - the name of the method to remove

    • bound

      public boolean bound(Object listener, String name)
      Returns true if the given function is currently bound to this event, or false if it is not. Use this to avoid binding the same function twice.
      Parameters:
      listener - the object that owns the method
      name - the name of the method to check
      Returns:
      true if bound, false if not
